activities_projects.title: Biosphere Equilibrium Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Model the delicate balance required for the Biosphere to exist
materials: Three overlapping transparent circles (Aviation, Hydro, Litho)
steps: Identify specific geographical regions where the Biosphere is extremely thin (e.g., high mountains or deep ocean trenches)
Calculate the percentage of Earth's total radius that the Biosphere actually occupies
Simulate a 'collapse' in one domain (e.g., severe air pollution in the Atmosphere) and map the ripple effects on the other two
Explain why life cannot exist if any one domain is removed entirely
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the mathematical and biological rarity of the Biosphere zone
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision in identifying 'Life Zones' and depth of the domino-effect analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Anthropogenic Modification Audit
activities_projects.objective: Quantify the extent of human interference in a local ecosystem
steps: Compare satellite images of a 5km radius around your school from 2010 vs. 2025
Calculate the 'Percentage of Natural Cover Lost' to human-made components (roads, buildings)
Research the 'Heat Island Effect' caused by these modifications
Draft a technical proposal for 'Green Corridors' to restore the natural balance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the long-term impact of human activities on the Lithosphere and local climate
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of land-use calculations and feasibility of the restoration proposal (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ecosystem Energy Flow Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Trace the complex movement of energy through Biotic and Abiotic components
steps: Select a complex local ecosystem (e.g., a wetland or forest)
Map not just a food chain, but a 'Food Web' including decomposers and scavengers
Include Abiotic inputs like Solar Energy and Mineral Nutrients from the Lithosphere
Explain the 10% Law of energy transfer between trophic levels
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the thermodynamic laws governing life within an environment
activities_projects.evaluation: Complexity of the web and accuracy of energy flow descriptions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Atmospheric Composition and Global Change
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how minor changes in Abiotic gas ratios affect the Biotic world
materials: Data on CO2 levels over the last 100 years
steps: Analyze the correlation between industrial growth (Human Environment) and CO2 concentration (Atmosphere)
Calculate the projected temperature rise if CO2 levels double
Discuss the impact of this change on the Hydrosphere (sea-level rise) and Lithosphere (soil erosion)
Write a scientific abstract on 'The Interconnectedness of Global Environmental Domains'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Links human economic activities to global-scale Abiotic shifts
activities_projects.evaluation: Data interpretation skills and scientific synthesis in the abstract (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Economic Environment Evolution Study
activities_projects.objective: Deconstruct the shift from 'Needs' to 'Wants' in human history
materials: Case studies of the Industrial Revolution
steps: Compare the resource consumption of a 12th-century village vs. a 21st-century smart city
Analyze how the 'Information Revolution' has created a new 'Digital Environment'
Evaluate the concept of 'Sustainable Development' as a bridge between development and nature
Debate: 'Is it possible to revert to a state of perfect harmony with nature in the 21st century?'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically examines the trajectory of human-environment interactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of historical comparison and strength of the sustainability argument (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Lithosphere Nutrient Cycling Research
activities_projects.objective: Understand the chemical interaction between soil and life
materials: Samples from various sites (Farm, Forest, Construction site)
steps: Test the N-P-K (Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium) levels of different soil samples
Correlate the Biotic diversity of the site with its Abiotic soil quality
Explain how the Lithosphere acts as a reservoir for nutrients essential to the Biosphere
Discuss the impact of chemical fertilizers on this natural cycle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the biochemical dependency of life on the hard crust of the Earth
activities_projects.evaluation: Correlation of test results with environmental observations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hydrosphere: The Blue Economy Project
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the human environment's reliance on water for trade and survival
materials: Research on desalination and hydroelectric power
steps: Map the world's major 'Maritime Trade Routes' and explain their importance to the human environment
Calculate the water footprint of a common product (like a pair of jeans)
Research how 'Water Scarcity' can lead to social and political conflicts in the Human Environment
Present a case for 'Water Literacy' in schools
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects the physical Hydrosphere to global economics and geopolitics
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of the 'Water Footprint' analysis and socio-political insights (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Bio-geochemical Cycle Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Model the continuous movement of matter through the environment
steps: Select either the Carbon or Nitrogen cycle
Trace an atom as it moves from the Atmosphere (gas) to the Lithosphere (solid) to the Biosphere (organic)
Identify 'Human Intervention Points' where the cycle is disrupted (e.g., fossil fuel burning or deforestation)
Propose methods to 'Close the Loop' and restore the cycle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes the concept of 'Environment' as a dynamic, recycling system
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical accuracy of the cycle and identification of disruption points (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) Roleplay
activities_projects.objective: Apply environmental logic to high-level decision making
materials: Scenario: A proposal to build an airport in a forested Lithosphere area near a river
steps: Assign roles: Hydrologist, Ecologist, Urban Planner, Local Resident, and Industrialist
Conduct a 'Public Hearing' to assess the impact on all four domains (L, H, A, B)
Use the 'Precautionary Principle' to decide if the project should proceed
Write a formal 'Impact Statement' summarizing the environmental risks
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates the complexity of maintaining balance in a modified human environment
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking and use of domain-specific terminology (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ecological Footprint Calculator
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the personal impact on the Earth's environment
steps: Track your household’s energy use, waste production, and dietary habits for one week
Convert this data into 'Global Hectares' (GHA) required to support your lifestyle
Compare your footprint with the Earth’s biocapacity per person
Formulate a 'Personal Environment Policy' to reduce your footprint by 20%
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Internalizes the concept of personal responsibility in environmental sustainability
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the calculation and feasibility of the reduction plan (10 marks)
